macro_segments.title: Introduction and Overview of Shelo Lishmah Debate

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks and daf orientation
Review of previous day's discussion on degrees of shelo lishmah

macro_segments.title: The Debate Between Rabbi Yochanan and Reish Lakish on Machshava of Future Avodah

micro_segments.title: Case of shechita with intent for future zrika shelo lishmah
Rabbi Yochanan's position: machshavos from one avodah to another are valid
Rashi's explanation of piggul as a model and its limitations
Reish Lakish's position: machshavos do not transfer between avodos
Distinction between piggul and shelo lishmah regarding avodah classification

macro_segments.title: Avodas Zarah Case and the Parallel Debate Structure

micro_segments.title: Case of shechita with intent to offer to avodas zarah via future zrika
Rabbi Yochanan's position on avodas zarah case
Reish Lakish's position on avodas zarah case
Gemara's explanation of why both cases are necessary

macro_segments.title: Rav Yirmiya's Proof for Rabbi Yochanan Using Piggul Kal V'Chomer

micro_segments.title: Introduction of Rav Dimi's report of the debate continuation in Eretz Yisrael
Rav Yirmiya supports Rabbi Yochanan using piggul model
Kal v'chomer: if thought about future eating invalidates, so should thought about future zrika

macro_segments.title: Rava bar Ahilai's Challenge to Piggul Proof

micro_segments.title: Challenge: piggul involves kares, whereas shelo lishmah does not
Kal v'chomer using chutz limkomo instead of piggul

macro_segments.title: Rav Ashi's Improved Kal V'Chomer Using Chutz Limkomo

micro_segments.title: Chutz limkomo parallel: thought about wrong place invalidates like thought about wrong time
Kal v'chomer from chutz limkomo to shelo lishmah

macro_segments.title: Rav Pappa's Challenge to Rav Ashi and the Tosafta's Interpretation

micro_segments.title: Rav Pappa argues chutz limkomo is weightier and affects all korbanos
Shelo lishmah only affects pesach and chatas, therefore not as weighty

macro_segments.title: Rav Ashi's Refined Proof Using Shelo Lishem Baalim Model

micro_segments.title: Explanation of shelo lishem baalim versus shelo lishmah distinction
Shelo lishem baalim requires machshava about zrika during earlier avodah
Kal v'chomer: if thought about zrika for wrong person invalidates, so for wrong purpose

macro_segments.title: Rav Ila's Defense of Reish Lakish Using Textual Analysis

micro_segments.title: Why Torah mentions zrika separately if kal v'chomer works
Interpretation: extra word limits rather than expands shelo lishmah at zrika
Gemara's challenge: maybe the word expands rather than limits
Gemara's response: Torah's extra mention supports Reish Lakish by limiting

macro_segments.title: Rav Nachman and Rava's Position on the Debate

micro_segments.title: Rav Nachman follows Rabbi Yochanan position
Rava initially follows Reish Lakish then retracts to Rav Nachman
Rava's retraction based on Rav Ashi's strong kal v'chomer

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Eliezer's Position on Asham and Chatas Parallelism

micro_segments.title: Mishna context: Rabbi Eliezer says asham is like chatas
Rabbi Eliezer's braisas: chatas comes for sin, asham comes for sin, both should be shelo lishmah

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehoshua's First Response: Blood Location Distinction

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehoshua: chatas blood goes above line, asham below line
Rabbi Eliezer counters with pesach blood going below line
Rabbi Yehoshua: pesach has fixed time, different characteristic
Rabbi Eliezer: neither characteristic applies to all cases, back to chatas
Rabbi Yehoshua's response implies going in circles, needs common denominator

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Eliezer's Hirhurim Proof Using the Word Hu

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Eliezer's textual proof from word hu appearing for all three
Rabbi Yehoshua: hu by chatas appears at shechita, hu by asham appears after emurim
Asham hu appears after emurim burning, too late to invalidate

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Eliezer's Explicit Linkage Proof from Pesach-Asham Verse

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Eliezer: Torah explicitly links asham to chatas with ka-chatas ka-asham
No response recorded from Rabbi Yehoshua to this third proof

macro_segments.title: Gemara's Analysis of the Tzad Hashaveh Logic

micro_segments.title: Gemara explains Rabbi Yehoshua's going-in-circles response as setting up common denominator
Explanation of tzad hashaveh: eliminate distinguishing features to find commonality
Gemara suggests underlying concern: tzad hashaveh logic limited by kares association

macro_segments.title: Gemara's Question Why Blood Location Matters and Alternative Distinctions

micro_segments.title: Why focus on above-below line rather than other weightinesses of chatas
Alternative distinctions: four applications, finger vs throwing, horns
Gemara: multiple ways to show chatas weightier, he picked one of several

macro_segments.title: Abbaye's Explanation: Why Asham Blood Cannot Go Above Line

micro_segments.title: Why didn't Rabbi Eliezer say asham blood goes above line like chatas
Kal v'chomer from olah: olah is entirely burned yet blood goes below
Olah doesn't atone, so chatas being above-line not from atonement
Chatat ha-of is below line and atones, so not disqualifying for asham
But bird is not a regular animal, so olah-asham common denominator
Kodshei kodashim status of both olah and pesach shows below-line applies to weighty items

macro_segments.title: Debate Over Word Osah and Its Limiting Scope

micro_segments.title: Rava from Pizka's challenge: asham is weightier so should go above line
Explanation: word osah in Torah limits blood placement above line to chatas only
Gemara: doesn't osah also exclude everything for lishmah requirement
Answer: osah for lishmah doesn't exclude because pesach also requires lishmah
But osah for blood placement excludes because only chatas among slaughtered animals goes above

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Eliezer B'Rabbi Shimon's Position on Separate Placement

micro_segments.title: Alternative explanation: Rabbi Eliezer relies on Rabbi Eliezer B'Rabbi Shimon
Rabbi Eliezer B'Rabbi Shimon: each korban needs separate place on altar
Olas ha-of has specific place, chatas has specific corners, leaving no place for asham above
Conclusion: asham blood must go below line due to placement constraints

macro_segments.title: Summary and Unresolved Tension

micro_segments.title: Gemara explanation of why Rabbi Eliezer concedes asham blood below line
Rabbi Eliezer still maintains asham should be shelo lishmah like chatas despite blood location
Debate continues tomorrow
